session_handoff.purpose: Fresh-session boot prompt for a new Claude Code window. Paste this file path into the prompt: 'Read C:/ClaudeSkills/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-02-graph-compare-viewer-session-handoff.json then follow instructions_for_claude exactly.'

instructions_for_claude.step_1: Read this entire JSON before responding.
instructions_for_claude.step_2: Invoke 'superpowers:brainstorming' skill FIRST — do not respond before invoking it.
instructions_for_claude.step_3: After skill loads, announce: 'Graphify Sub-project 1 — Graph Compare Viewer. Booting from session handoff v2.0. All architecture decisions locked. Entering plan mode.'
instructions_for_claude.step_4: Invoke 'superpowers:writing-plans' to produce the implementation plan — architecture is already approved, skip directly to plan writing.
instructions_for_claude.step_5: The plan must use 'superpowers:subagent-driven-development' execution model — break into discrete agent tasks with explicit file ownership per task.
instructions_for_claude.step_6: After plan is written and approved by Marcus, invoke 'superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents' to spin up the agent team.
instructions_for_claude.step_7: Agents execute under 'superpowers:executing-plans' — each agent owns one task, validates its own output, marks complete before next agent starts.
instructions_for_claude.step_8: Before any agent writes code, load 'universal-coding-standards' skill from C:/ClaudeSkills/skills/universal-coding-standards/.
instructions_for_claude.step_9: Do NOT begin implementing until Marcus approves the written plan.
instructions_for_claude.step_10: Skills location: C:/ClaudeSkills — all skills Marcus needs are installed there.

developer_profile.name: Marcus Daley
developer_profile.background: Navy veteran (9 years leadership), Full Sail BS Online Game Development (Feb 6 2026), stay-at-home dad
developer_profile.focus: Portfolio-grade software — AAA studio practices applied universally. Quality over speed. Never quick-and-dirty.
developer_profile.key_rule: Never suggest minimal or simplified designs. Always research the most advanced, sophisticated approach.
developer_profile.commit_rule: Never include Co-Authored-By lines referencing Claude or Anthropic. Marcus pushes manually.

shared_data_contracts.report_input: GRAPH_REPORT.md (Sub-project 2 will upgrade to GRAPH_REPORT.json — design panel to accept either)

locked_decisions.approach: Approach B — split-pane with resizable divider. Locked.
locked_decisions.rendering_model: Single rendering host — NOT iframes. graph-compare.html owns both graph renders directly. Eliminates cross-frame localStorage/Canvas/pointer-event isolation problems.
locked_decisions.renderer_abstraction: GraphRenderer interface required from day one. vis-network is the temporary impl. Sub-project 4 (Sigma.js) replaces only the four method bodies. Interface: init(container, graphData, options), destroy(), fit(), on(event, cb).
locked_decisions.data_loading: JSON-first. Loads graph.json and graph-original.json via fetch() at runtime. No embedded RAW_NODES/RAW_EDGES globals.
locked_decisions.original_graph_recovery: scripts/extract_original_graph.py pulls graph.json from git ref (default HEAD~2, configurable via CLI arg) and writes graph-original.json. One-time operation. graph-original.json is gitignored.
locked_decisions.markdown_rendering: No CDN. No marked.js. Inline minimal markdown parser (<150 lines) handles headings, tables, bold, code blocks — enough for GRAPH_REPORT format.
locked_decisions.annotation_system: Floating drawer (right-edge, slides in/out via tab handle). Reads/writes localStorage in compare tool's own origin. Copy All Notes produces clean plain text for pasting into Claude.
locked_decisions.vis_network_cdn: vis-network loaded from unpkg CDN is acceptable — compare tool is a dev tool, not a client deliverable. CDN acceptable here only.
locked_decisions.no_graph_html_dependency: graph-compare.html does NOT depend on graph.html. They are independent applications. graph.html continues to exist as a standalone viewer.
locked_decisions.sub_project_order: 1 (Compare Viewer) → 2 (Bundle Generator) → 3 (Brainstorm + PRD) → 4 (Sigma.js). Strict. No parallel building.

output_files: graphify-out/graph-compare.html — single standalone HTML file, full rendering host
graphify-out/graph.json — canonical fixed graph data (already exists)
graphify-out/graph-original.json — pre-fix graph data (generated by extraction script, gitignored)
graphify-out/GRAPH_REPORT.md — rendered inline by compare tool (already exists)

internal_layers.responsibility: CSS flex: left pane, draggable divider, right pane. Pointer-event divider drag. Viewport-filling height calc.
Button strip toggling right pane between Original Graph render and GRAPH_REPORT panel.
Thin abstraction over vis-network. Both left and right graph panels instantiate independently. Four methods: init, destroy, fit, on.
Renders GRAPH_REPORT.md to HTML. No CDN. Handles headings, tables, bold, inline code, fenced code blocks.
Fixed-position right-edge sidebar. localStorage persistence. Add/delete notes. Copy All Notes button.

approved_architecture.data_flow: fetch(graph.json) + fetch(graph-original.json) on load → GraphRenderer.init(leftContainer, fixedData) + GraphRenderer.init(rightContainer, originalData) → both panels render independently → user interacts via split-pane, switcher, and annotation drawer

acceptance_criteria: All three panels accessible without page reload (left: fixed graph, right: toggles between original graph and GRAPH_REPORT)
Annotations persist in localStorage across browser sessions
Copy All Notes produces clean plain text for pasting into Claude
Standalone HTML — opens directly from filesystem (file:// protocol), no server required
GraphRenderer abstraction in place — vis-network never called directly from layout code
No external dependencies except vis-network CDN (dev tool exception)
graph-original.json extracted correctly from git history by the Python script

brainstorm_artifact_instructions.what: The interactive brainstorm artifact is Sub-project 3 — it is NOT built in this session. However, the compare viewer's architecture must not block it. When Sub-project 3 arrives, the brainstorm component will be a React single-file component embedded in the client bundle.
brainstorm_artifact_instructions.standard: Must fully adhere to Brainstorm Artifact UI/UX Standard — load skill at C:/ClaudeSkills/skills/brainstorm-artifact/ when Sub-project 3 begins.
brainstorm_artifact_instructions.current_session_scope: Sub-project 1 only. Do not scope-creep into Sub-project 3 brainstorm UI.

agent_team_model.ownership_rule: Each agent owns exactly one task and one set of files. No overlapping file edits.

suggested_agent_breakdown.owns: graph-compare.html skeleton: HTML structure, CSS flex layout, draggable divider, viewport height, dark theme (#0f0f1a bg matching graph.html)
suggested_agent_breakdown.deliverable: Renders two empty panes with working drag divider. No graph data yet.

suggested_agent_breakdown.owns: GraphRenderer object inside graph-compare.html: init, destroy, fit, on methods wrapping vis-network. fetch() calls for graph.json and graph-original.json.
suggested_agent_breakdown.deliverable: Both panes render graph data. Left = fixed, Right = original. No switcher yet.

suggested_agent_breakdown.owns: Button strip above right pane toggling between Original Graph and GRAPH_REPORT. Inline markdown parser rendering GRAPH_REPORT.md. fetch() for GRAPH_REPORT.md.
suggested_agent_breakdown.deliverable: Right pane correctly switches between graph view and styled report view.

suggested_agent_breakdown.owns: Sliding right-edge annotation drawer. Add note, delete note, Copy All Notes. localStorage persistence key: 'graphify-compare-annotations'.
suggested_agent_breakdown.deliverable: Drawer opens/closes, notes persist across page reload, Copy All Notes outputs clean text.

suggested_agent_breakdown.owns: scripts/extract_original_graph.py: pulls graph.json from configurable git ref, writes graphify-out/graph-original.json. CLI arg --ref defaults to HEAD~2.
suggested_agent_breakdown.deliverable: Script runs, graph-original.json is valid JSON matching graph.json schema.

suggested_agent_breakdown.owns: End-to-end acceptance criteria verification. Runs extraction script, opens graph-compare.html, walks all 8 acceptance criteria, documents results.
suggested_agent_breakdown.deliverable: Signed-off validation report. All 8 criteria pass or blockers documented.
